category_title: Students with mild intellectual disabilities exhibiting academic engagement, off-task, or disruptive problem behaviors
scenario_title: Off-task behavior during reading and writing tasks
minimal_context: The student is copying sentences from the board into their notebook during a classroom activity.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Please copy the sentences from the board into your notebook.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Looks at the board, but then starts playing with pencils instead of writing.)

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Stops looking at the board, starts looking around the classroom, and attends to other things irrelevant to the lesson.)

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Turns completely away from the notebook and looks outside through the window, continuing to play with the pencil without copying anything.)

dialogue_combined:
TUTOR: Please copy the sentences from the board into your notebook.

STUDENT: (Looks at the board, but then starts playing with pencils instead of writing.)

TUTOR: Keep trying to write.

STUDENT: (Stops looking at the board, starts looking around the classroom, and attends to other things irrelevant to the lesson.)

TUTOR: Focus on your notebook.

STUDENT: (Turns completely away from the notebook and looks outside through the window, continuing to play with the pencil without copying anything.)

task.content: Writing, listening to teacher or peer, copying in her/his notebook from what is written on the board, and reading aloud or silently during Turkish and Social Studies courses

challenging_behavior_trigger.condition: Teachers not sufficiently engaging these students in classroom activities
challenging_behavior_trigger.observable_behavior: Looking around, looking outside through the window, playing with pencils, or attending to other things irrelevant to the lesson

behavior_patterns.condition: When teachers do not make adjustments in the instructional program toward them
behavior_patterns.observable_behavior: Behavior that does not disturb others nor interrupts lessons but which also cannot be coded as academic engagement behavior
